intro: Humboldtia pusilla, also known as the "Indian Madhuca", is a plant species native to the tropical forests of India. It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can aid in treating skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Humboldtia pusilla include alkaloids, flavonoids, and triterpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Humboldtia pusilla is commonly prepared into herbal teas, decoctions, and ointments to harness its benefits, with the most common preparations being Madhuca oil, a medicated oil used for skin and wound care.
